gaps: the official CLI is optimized for human terminal use rather than autonomous MCP hosts
the official CLI does not provide a local archive/search layer for agent context
the official CLI does not emit explicit approval-ready money-movement plans before writes
patterns: multiple output formats and GJSON transforms

novel_features.name: Agent money movement plan
novel_features.description: Builds a read-only approval plan with body, idempotency key, dry-run command, and execute command before payment or transfer writes.
novel_features.rationale: Banking APIs are high-risk for agents; separating planning from execution makes approval and audit explicit.
novel_features.example: mercury-pp-cli workflow payment-plan --kind transfer --source-account-id acct_src --destination-account-id acct_dst --amount 25 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can prepare exact write commands without moving money.

novel_features.name: Agent-native local archive
novel_features.description: Syncs supported Mercury resources into a local SQLite store for offline search and analytics.
novel_features.rationale: Agents need stable local context instead of repeatedly polling account and transaction endpoints.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reduces API calls and gives agents repeatable context.

novel_features.name: Structured command introspection
novel_features.description: Emits machine-readable command metadata for agents and MCP hosts.
novel_features.rationale: Agents need a compact tool map rather than scraping human help text.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Improves autonomous command selection and reduces context waste.
